name: Oxfam America
mission:
Oxfam is a global organization that fights inequality to end poverty and injustice. We offer lifesaving
support in times of crisis and advocate for economic justice, gender equality, and climate action. We
demand equal rights and equal treatment so that everyone can thrive, not just survive. The future is
equal.
theoryOfChange: Oxfam America has a published 'Oxfam America 2030 Strategy Framework' which guides their work and outlines their goals to fight inequality and achieve systemic change. This framework, approved in March 2020, details their approach to evolving issues and how they will operate, including open and collaborative partnerships and rapid experimentation. Additionally, Oxfam America has invested significantly in a Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL) system since 2006, with a dedicated central unit (LEAD) and staff. This system ensures that all long-term programs, major campaigns, and key innovation projects have a rigorous MEL system, including baselines, monitoring reports, and annual reviews to document progress and facilitate learning. Oxfam America has also piloted a 'Corporate Inequality Framework' (CIF) to help investors analyze how companies contribute to inequality, which is described as a methodological and holistic framework.

scoreSummary: Oxfam AmericaHQ shows good impact and good alignment.

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 3.6% of revenue ($2,733,598 on $75,339,434)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue >$50M, CEO pay above 1% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

components.evidence: Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 34/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, humanitarian cause area

components.evidence: Board: 22 members, but For orgs with revenue >$50M, CEO pay above 1% of revenue warrants scrutiny. — oversight concern
components.name: Governance

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: CEO compensation is 3.6% of revenue ($2,733,598 on $75,339,434)

risks.description: CEO compensation is 3.6% of revenue ($2,733,598 on $75,339,434)

score_details.score_summary: Oxfam AmericaHQ shows good impact and good alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly)

all_citations.claim: Oxfam America holds a Charity Navigator score of 99.33333333333333/100 and holds 8.0 months of working capital.
The organization operates programs in international relief, emergency response, and economic justice.
all_citations.source_name: Download our most recent financial audit

all_citations.claim: Oxfam's programs have successfully improved media competence and influenced laws through CSO input in Ghana.
all_citations.source_name: Download NORAD Ghana Final Project Evaluation Report

all_citations.claim: The organization facilitated the adoption of CSO-proposed amendments by the Tanzanian parliament.
all_citations.source_name: Download NORAD Tanzania Final Project Evaluation Report

all_citations.claim: Oxfam works to empower poor people to play a central role in development and accepts general charitable donations.
The organization reported total revenue of $75,339,434 and a program expense ratio of 72.3%.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Oxfam America earns a high rating due to its exceptional transparency and direct service impact, though its overall score is moderated by fundraising efficiency and program expense ratios.
areas_for_improvement: High fundraising costs relative to revenue, spending $0.21 per $1 raised
program expense ratio of 72.3% is lower than some top-tier peer organizations

dimension_explanations.alignment: Oxfam America offers a high donor fit for those seeking to support urgent humanitarian causes through sadaqah [6]. Its focus on economic justice and emergency response aligns with the needs of vulnerable populations, supported by a robust financial position including 8.0 months of working capital [1][2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high impact through direct service and systemic change, such as successfully influencing parliamentary amendments in Tanzania and improving media competence in Ghana [3][5]. With a total revenue of $75,339,434, the charity maintains a program expense ratio of 72.3% and demonstrates high data confidence in its reported outcomes [1][7].

baseline_narrative.headline: Oxfam America empowers marginalized communities through local partnerships and systemic advocacy to combat poverty and inequality.
strengths: Exceptional financial health and accountability with a Charity Navigator score of 99.33333333333333/100
Strong operational stability with 8.0 months of working capital
Proven track record in legislative advocacy and civil society capacity building
baseline_narrative.summary: Oxfam America is a global organization that works with local partners to provide international relief, emergency response, and economic justice programs [2]. The organization focuses on empowering poor people to lead development within their own communities and regions [6].

all_citations.claim: overall score of 99.99.33333333333333/100 from Charity Navigator

all_citations.quote: Oxfam is a global organization that fights inequality to end poverty and injustice.

all_citations.claim: Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid
local partners across Africa, Asia, and the Americas
all_citations.quote: Areas Served: Africa, North America, Europe, Asia, South America
empowering poor people to play a central role in the development

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Oxfam America earns a high rating due to its exceptional transparency and direct service impact, though its overall score is moderated by fundraising efficiency and program expense ratios.

areas_for_improvement.context: The <cite id="16">program expense ratio of 72.3%</cite> is lower than the <cite id="17">peer median of 90.3%</cite> for humanitarian organizations.
For an organization of this size, <cite id="18">officer compensation totaling $2,733,598</cite> warrants continued donor scrutiny regarding overhead allocation.

bbb_assessment.summary: Meets all standards for charity accountability with audited financial statements.

case_against.mitigation_notes: Oxfam mitigates these costs through high-impact advocacy that creates systemic change beyond direct cash transfers.
risk_factors: <cite id="17">Program ratio significantly below the 90.3% peer median</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="16">72.3% program expense ratio</cite>, which suggests a higher portion of funds is diverted to administration compared to peers.

confidence.notes: Data is exceptionally current and verified by multiple third parties.

alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment by <cite id="22">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> that currently have limited nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust Oxfam due to its <cite id="1">84-year track record</cite>, <cite id="14">fully independent board</cite>, and <cite id="3">Platinum-level data sharing</cite> with Candid.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility further, the organization could address the <cite id="18">high executive pay relative to revenue</cite> to align more closely with lean non-profit benchmarks.

impact.explanation: Oxfam demonstrates high efficiency in reach, with a <cite id="19">cost per beneficiary of $79.01</cite>, which is considered excellent for complex humanitarian work.
impact.improvement: Impact could be improved by increasing the <cite id="16">program expense ratio from 72.3%</cite> toward the industry standard of 75% or higher.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade A - Verified outcomes and external evaluations

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Empowering local organizations to lead development and advocacy

rich_narrative.headline: Oxfam America empowers marginalized communities through local partnerships and systemic advocacy to combat poverty and inequality.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value systemic change and advocacy alongside emergency relief, and who prefer supporting established global institutions with high transparency.
donor_motivations: Want to support long-term solutions to poverty, not just temporary aid
Value high-level accountability and third-party verified ratings
Interested in supporting local civil society empowerment in the Global South
giving_considerations: Donations are Sadaqah-eligible but not verified for Zakat
Organization has a large administrative footprint due to its advocacy and research arms
Revenue has seen a recent decline, suggesting a need for new funding streams

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade A based on <cite id="8">verified outcome metrics</cite> and multiple <cite id="26">external evaluation reports</cite> from donors like NORAD.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Oxfam uses an integrated approach combining <cite id="2">emergency response, economic justice, and gender equality</cite> to break the cycle of poverty.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that advocacy efforts actually result in legislative and social changes for the poor.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="23">-9.1% revenue CAGR</cite>, the organization has significant capacity to absorb new funding to restore previous program scales.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Oxfam is distinguished by its <cite id="24">massive grantmaking capacity</cite>, distributing over <cite id="25">$71 million in foreign grants</cite> to local partners.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Similar humanitarian scope with different donor base
similar_organizations.name: Baitulmaal, Inc.

strengths.detail: Oxfam holds <cite id="4">a near-perfect 99.33/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="3">Platinum Seal from Candid</cite>, indicating the highest level of transparency.
The organization maintains <cite id="11">8.0 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring operational stability during global crises.
Beyond direct aid, Oxfam has successfully seen <cite id="12">proposed amendments adopted by parliament</cite> through its partner networks in Tanzania.

strengths_deep_dive: Oxfam America distinguishes itself through a <cite id="13">direct service delivery model</cite> that prioritizes local empowerment over top-down aid. Their financial health is robust, with <cite id="14">100% independent board members</cite> providing rigorous oversight. Furthermore, their fundraising efficiency is notable, spending only <cite id="15">$0.21 per $1 raised</cite> according to recent filings, which is highly competitive for a large-scale humanitarian organization.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">1942</cite>, Oxfam America is a global humanitarian organization dedicated to <cite id="2">combating poverty and inequality</cite> through a dual approach of direct emergency response and systemic advocacy. The organization operates with a <cite id="3">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and maintains <cite id="4">an overall score of 99.99.33333333333333/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting exceptional accountability standards. In the 2024 fiscal year, Oxfam reported <cite id="5">total revenue of $83,115,051</cite>, which supports a vast network of <cite id="6">local partners across Africa, Asia, and the Americas</cite>. Unlike many traditional relief agencies, Oxfam emphasizes <cite id="7">civil society capacity building</cite>, ensuring that marginalized communities lead their own development. Their impact is evidenced by <cite id="8">verified outcome tracking</cite>, including successful legislative influence such as <cite id="9">passing laws with CSO input</cite> in regions like Ghana. While the organization is a major player in international relief, it operates as a <cite id="10">Sadaqah-eligible entity</cite>, focusing on broad humanitarian justice rather than specific religious tax compliance.
